title: Align install path of podman to the systemd service files
body:
`make && make install`  puts the podman executable in /usr/local/bin and installs systemd service files.  The latter have assumed, that the podman executable is at /usr/bin/podman .

Closes https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11858.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dilyanpalauzov* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **rhatdan** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @rhatdan` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Please refer to #11788, which was closed WONTFIX.

This is a hard problem. A simpleminded substitution of `/usr/local` is not the right approach. I recommend rejecting this PR.

> Please refer to #11788, which was closed WONTFIX.
> 
> This is a hard problem. A simpleminded substitution of `/usr/local` is not the right approach. I recommend rejecting this PR.

I concur.  This PR clearly conflicts with my statement in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11858; we're exchanging one problem for another.

I do not see any compelling reason at https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11788. I do not see why I was asked to open a PR, if the outcome was foreseenable from a ticket I opened.  At the end it is unclear, why those unknown organizations, using the unmodified service files directly, cannot alter the distributed podman.service file.  In particular, the unknown distributors of the unmodified podman.service file have not raised any objection.

A different fix to the “installed by `make install` systemd service files are broken” would be not to install the service files on “make install”.  I would also be fine with this way.

I think finding consensus on this PR is hopeless.
I don't see why we can't have the make install actually sed the unit files to match the path of the podman binary.  Why is this such a big issue?
